children.value: ), we obtained a calibration for an effective equation of state -- a relation between the effective pressure and density

children.html: Peff=ρσeff2P_{\rm eff}=\rho\sigma_{\rm eff}^2Peff​=ρσeff2​

children.value: . We provided simple power-law fitting results using two characterizations. (1) The relation between the volume-weighted averages of the midplane total pressure and density of the warm and cold gas (

children.html: Ptot,2pP_{\rm tot,2p}Ptot,2p​
nH,2p=ρ2p/1.4mHn_{\rm H, 2p}=\rho_{\rm 2p}/1.4m_HnH,2p​=ρ2p​/1.4mH​

children.value: in Table 2 of OK22). (2) The relation between the mass-weighted vertical velocity dispersion of the warm and cold phase and the dynamical equilibrium pressure (

children.html: σ‾eff,2p\overline{\sigma}_{\rm eff,2p}σeff,2p​
PDEP_{\rm DE}PDE​

children.value: in Table 2 of OK22). From (1), we calculated the midplane effective velocity dispersion

children.html: σeff,mid≡(Ptot,2p/ρ2p)1/2\sigma_{\rm eff,mid}\equiv(P_{\rm tot,2p}/\rho_{\rm 2p})^{1/2}σeff,mid​≡(Ptot,2p​/ρ2p​)1/2

children.value: . From (2), we calculated the mass-weighted average density

children.html: ρavg=PDE/σ‾eff,2p2\rho_{\rm avg}=P_{\rm DE}/\overline{\sigma}_{\rm eff,2p}^2ρavg​=PDE​/σeff,2p2​

children.value: and the press-density relation. Therefore, both provide same information; they just characterized differently.
In what follows, we will use

children.html: PeffP_{\rm eff}Peff​
nHn_HnH​

children.value: for effective pressure and number density. We do not distinguish

children.html: Ptot,2pP_{\rm tot,2p}Ptot,2p​
PDEP_{\rm DE}PDE​
Wtot\mathcal{W}_{\rm tot}Wtot​

children.value: as they are proven to be consistent with each other. We will use the nomenclature

children.html: σ‾eff,2p→σeff,avg\overline{\sigma}_{\rm eff,2p}\to\sigma_{\rm eff,avg}σeff,2p​→σeff,avg​

children.value: to be consistent with the TIGRESS-NCR results.

The power-law fit of the effective equation of state will not valid to the arbitrary low pressure and density. We show the fitting results and actual simulation data points. We caution that especially at low pressure and density regimes, the effective velocity dispersions cannot be much lower than the warm medium sound speed (

children.html: ∼10km/s\sim 10{\rm km/s}∼10km/s

children.value: ). It would be safe (and more physically meaningful) to apply a velocity floor of this level to the effective velocity dispersion and corresponding effective pressure when adopting this result. See

children.value: In either case, we can first solve the cubic equation for a constant $\sigma_{\rm eff}$ to get $H_{\rm gas}$ and $P_{\rm tot}=W_{\rm tot}$. For the non-constant case, we then update $\sigma_{\rm eff}$ based on the new $P_{\rm tot}$ and then solve for $H_{\rm gas}$ and $P_{\rm tot}$ again. We iteratively solve until $H_{\rm gas}$ is converged.
